janosch.original_entry:
JANOSCH
Das Geheimnis des Herrn Josef. Geheimnisgeschichten. Bilder von Janosch. Weinheim Beltz Verlag. 1990. 57 S. TB.

balthus.original_entry:
FRANK WEDEKIND IB 1148
Mine-Haha oder Über die körperliche Erziehung der jungen Mädchen. Mit 'Nu aus miroir' von Balthus. Frankfurt Insel 1994. 95 S. Neuwertig.

novalis.original_entry:
NOVALIS
Werke, Tagebücher und Briefe Friedrich von Hardenbergs. Herausgegeben von Hans-Joachim Mähl und Richard Samuel.
Band 1. Das dichterische Werk, Tagebücher und Briefe. Herausgegeben von Richard Samuel. 768 S.
Band 2. Das philosophisch-theoretische Werk. Herausgegeben von Hans-Joachim Mähl. 855 S.
Band 3. Kommentar. Von Hans Jürgen Balmes. 800 S.
München Hanser 1978. Lizenzausgabe 1999 für die Wissenschaftliche Buchgesellschaft. OLn. m. Lesebändchen im Schuber. Mit Besitzervermerk. Neuwertig
novalis.notes: multiple_editions: The entry describes the original Hanser edition (München 1978) as the primary publication year, with a Lizenzausgabe 1999 for the Wissenschaftliche Buchgesellschaft as a secondary edition. The 1978 date is used as the main publication_year per cataloging convention; the 1999 Lizenzausgabe is recorded in the edition field.

raab.original_entry:
PUSCHKIN, Alexander Sergejewitsch
Gesammelte Werke in sechs Bänden. Herausgegeben von Raab. Berlin Aufbau 1973. 3. Auflage.
Gedichte. 494 S.
Poeme und Märchen. 416 S.
Eugen Onegin. Dramen. 469 S.
Romane und Novellen. 498 S.
Aufsätze und Tagebücher. 594 S.
Briefe. 560 S.
Originalleinenbände und OU. Sehr gut erhalten.
raab.notes: The editor is listed only as 'Raab' without a given name. missing_person flag set accordingly.

schwaiger.original_entry:
HRDLICKA, Alfred
Alfred Hrdlicka. Texte von Eisler, Schönwald, Schwaiger, Muschik, Fischer und anderen. München Moos 1969. 178 S. OLn.m.OU. Ein Blatt herausgerissen Mit Widmung des Künstlers.

kyselak.original_entry:
KYSELAK
Skizzen einer Fußreise durch Österreich. Herausgegeben von Gabriele Gofrriller und mit einem Vorwort von Gabriele Goffriller und Chico Klein. Mit Abb. Salzburg Jung und Jung 2009. 480 S. OPbd.m.OU und Lesebändchen. Neuwertig.
kyselak.notes: The editor's name is spelled inconsistently in the entry: 'Gofrriller' (first mention) vs. 'Goffriller' (second mention). The correct spelling 'Goffriller' has been used throughout, as it appears in the second mention and matches the known publication. The author KYSELAK (Josef Kyselak, 1799–1831) is given without a first name in the entry; no first name has been added.

groothuis.original_entry:
CORSO WIEN/ GROOTHUIS (Hg.)
Küss die Hand, Moderne. Gastgeberin Eva Menasse. Texte von Elfriede Jelinek, Alfred Dorfer, Thomas Glavinic u.a. Fotogrfien von Nikolaus Walter. Hamburg 2011. 157 S. OIPbd.
groothuis.notes: 1) Typo corrected: 'Fotogrfien' → 'Fotografien' (corrected_by_api). 2) Format notation 'OIPbd.' is unclear — 'OI' is not a standard cataloging abbreviation; possibly 'OPbd.' (Originalpappband) with a stray 'I', or a different binding type. Copied verbatim into format_original; expansion in format_expanded is a placeholder and should be verified (api_concerned). 3) No publisher is listed in the entry despite Hamburg being given as place of publication.

schneidawind.original_entry:
SCHNEIDAWIND
Leben Kaiser Josephs des Zweiten. Bearbeitet von … Mit dem getreuen Bildniß des Kaisers. Hamburg Berendsohn 1846. 112 S. OBrosch. gut erhalten.
schneidawind.notes: The entry contains 'Bearbeitet von …' indicating an editor or reviser whose name is missing (ellipsis in place of name). missing_person flag set accordingly.

stoob.original_entry:
STOOB
Festschrift anläßlich des 750-Jahr-Jubiläums und der Erhebung zur Marktgemeinde 1979. Mit zahlr. Fotografien. Eisenstadt 1979. 228 S. OLn. m. OU. Neuwertig.
stoob.notes:
mvc: infos in Bibliographie des Volkskundemuseums gefunden - Hrsg. Ladislaus FINTA. https://volkskundemuseum.at/jart/prj3/volkskundemuseum/data/publikation/1533519994948/1533519994948.pdf
The entry lists only 'STOOB' as the author/editor without a given name, and no publisher is provided. It is unclear whether 'STOOB' refers to a person (e.g., Heinz Stoob, a known German historian) or is part of a place name or institution. The festschrift context and the single surname suggest this may be an incomplete or abbreviated entry. Human verification recommended to identify the full name and publisher.

ludwig.original_entry:
KLABUND
Literaturgeschichte. Die deutsche und die fremde Dichtung von den Anfängen bis zur Gegenwart. Neugeordnet und ergänzt von Ludwig
Wien Phaidon-Verlag 1930. 398 S. Mit Abbildungen. OLn.
ludwig.notes: mvc: mehrere Ausgaben um die Zeit gefunden, sollte wohl Ludwig Goldschneider sein. The entry states 'Neugeordnet und ergänzt von Ludwig' but only a first name 'Ludwig' is given — the surname of the editor is missing. missing_person flag set accordingly.

haarmann.original_entry:
HAARMANN,
Universalgeschichte der Schrift. Frankfurt Campus 1998. 576 S. Sonderausgabe Parkland. Verlag. Mit zahlreichen Abb. OPbd.
haarmann.notes: Author's given name is missing from the entry. The author is likely Harald Haarmann, a well-known German linguist who wrote 'Universalgeschichte der Schrift', but the given name has not been inserted to avoid incorrect attribution.

graesse.original_entry:
GESTA ROMANORUM
Das älteste Märchen- und Legendenbuch des christlichen Mittelalters. Nach der Übersetzung von Graesse ausgewählt von Hermann Hesse. 4.-7. Tausend. Leipzig Insel o. J. 285 S. Buntes OLn. mit Rückenvergoldung. Sehr gut erhalten.2. Ex.
graesse.notes: The entry states the text was selected by Hermann Hesse from the translation by Graesse (Johann Georg Theodor Grässe). Hesse's role is that of selector/editor rather than translator; Graesse is the translator. The publication year is given as 'o. J.' (ohne Jahr). The Insel Verlag Leipzig edition of this work is known, but the exact undated printing cannot be verified without further information. Graesse's full name is not given in the entry — only the surname appears.

julian.original_entry:
JULIAN
Briefe. Griechisch-deutsch ed. Berthold K. Weis. München Heimeran 1973. 366 S. OLn. m. OU. Verlagsfrisch Tusculum-Bücherei
julian.notes: The entry is a bilingual Greek-German edition, so is_translation is set to true with original_language Greek; however, no separate translator is named (the editor Berthold K. Weis likely prepared the German translation as well, but this is not explicitly stated). The author 'JULIAN' likely refers to Julian the Apostate (Roman Emperor, 4th century AD). The editor's name appears as 'Weis' — the known Tusculum editor of Julian's letters is 'Berthold K. Weis', which matches; no correction needed but flagged for verification.

nonnos.original_entry:
NONNOS
Dionysiaka. Verdeutscht von Thassilo von Scheffer. Wiesbaden Dieterich o.J. 1004 S. Goldlgeprägter Leinenband mit OU. Neuwertig.
nonnos.notes: Typo 'Goldlgeprägter' corrected to 'Goldgeprägter' in condition field. Publication year given as 'o.J.' (ohne Jahr / no year), so publication_year is null. The Dieterich'sche Verlagsbuchhandlung in Wiesbaden published this translation by Thassilo von Scheffer, consistent with known bibliographic records.

juvenal.original_entry:
JUVENAL
(Satyren) Übersetzt und mit Anmerkungen für Ungelehrte versehen von D. Carl Friedrich Bardt. Neue verbesserte Auflage. Leipzig 1810. X, 307 S. Mit Titelkupfer und Vignette.
AULUS PERSIUS FLACCUS
Satyren. Mit einer Einleitung und Erläuterungen versehen von Georg Gustav Fülleborn, Breslau. Wien und Prag bey Franz Haas 1803. Mit Titelkupfer und Vignette.
OHLdr. mit Rückentitel, dieser viel benutzt. Einige hs. Bemerkungen auf dem Schmutztitel. Text und Kupfer tadellos. Zus.
juvenal.notes: This entry describes two works bound together ('Zus.' = zusammengebunden): (1) Juvenal, Satyren, translated by D. Carl Friedrich Bardt, Leipzig 1810, X + 307 pp., with title copperplate and vignette; (2) Aulus Persius Flaccus, Satyren, introduced and annotated by Georg Gustav Fülleborn (of Breslau), Wien und Prag bey Franz Haas 1803, with title copperplate and vignette. The main record has been assigned to the Juvenal work as first described. The Persius volume is noted in condition. No publisher is given for the Juvenal volume. The format 'OHLdr.' (Originalhalbleder) applies to the combined binding. The second work is a separate publication and not a translation entry per se — its translator/editor status is ambiguous. Human verification recommended to determine whether two separate records should be created.

livius.original_entry:
LIVIUS
Römische Geschichte seit Gründung der Stadt. Band 1 – 2. A. d. Lateinischen von Heinrich Dittrich. Mit einer Karte. Berlin Aufbau Verlag 1978. XL, 522; 550 S. OLn. Neuwertig. Band 1-2

hausner.original_entry:
HAUSNER
Adam. Fotos und Bildgestaltung Manfred Bockelmann. Texte Friedrich Hacker, Rudolf Hausner, Gustav Rene Hocke. Stuttgart Belser 1974. 131 S. Mit zahlr. Abb. OLn.
hausner.notes: The entry lists 'HAUSNER' as the author with the title 'Adam'. Rudolf Hausner (1914–1995) was an Austrian Fantastic Realist painter, and 'Adam' is a known work/monograph associated with him. However, the entry structure is ambiguous: 'HAUSNER' appears as the cataloged author, yet 'Rudolf Hausner' also appears among the contributors providing text. It is possible that 'HAUSNER' as the main entry refers to Rudolf Hausner as the subject/artist of the monograph, with Bockelmann providing photos and Hacker, Hausner, and Hocke providing texts. Human verification is recommended to confirm the authorship role and whether the main entry should be treated as a monograph about Hausner rather than authored by him.

athanassiadiss.original_entry:
UHL, Ottokar
Achleitner, Athanassiadiss, Muck: Ottokar Uhl – Architekt und Ermöglicher. Mit einem Vorwort von Franz Kuzmich. Wien Picus 2002. 64 S. OPbd.
athanassiadiss.notes: The editors are listed with only surnames: 'Achleitner', 'Athanassiadiss', 'Muck'. 'Athanassiadiss' may be a misspelling (possibly 'Athanassiades' or similar), but no unambiguous correction can be made. Full given names for all three editors are absent. The subject of the book is Ottokar Uhl (architect), listed as the heading name — he is not the author but the subject.

muck.original_entry:
UHL, Ottokar
Achleitner, Athanassiadiss, Muck: Ottokar Uhl – Architekt und Ermöglicher. Mit einem Vorwort von Franz Kuzmich. Wien Picus 2002. 64 S. OPbd.
muck.notes: The editors are listed with only surnames: 'Achleitner', 'Athanassiadiss', 'Muck'. 'Athanassiadiss' may be a misspelling (possibly 'Athanassiades' or similar), but no unambiguous correction can be made. Full given names for all three editors are absent. The subject of the book is Ottokar Uhl (architect), listed as the heading name — he is not the author but the subject.

velazquez.original_entry:
VELAZQUEZ
Las Meninas. Der Essay von Michel Foucault. A.d.Französ. von Ulrich Köppen. Mit einer Kurzbiographie des Malers und farbigen Abbildungen. Nachwort von Rainer Marx. Frankfurt Insel 1999. 106 S. Mit Abb. it 2528
velazquez.notes: The entry lists VELAZQUEZ as the author, but the work described is an essay by Michel Foucault about Velázquez's painting 'Las Meninas'. Foucault is the actual author of the text; Velázquez is the subject/painter. The cataloging under VELAZQUEZ may reflect a subject heading rather than authorship. Human verification recommended to clarify authorship attribution.

thudydides.original_entry:
THUDYDIDES
Geschichte des Peloponnesischen Krieges. Deutsch von Joh. Dav. Heilmann. Berlin Lambert Schneider o.J. 723 S. Mit 2 Plänen in Kartentasche OLn. m. Lesebändchen. Bibliotheksstempel Gutes Exemplar.
thudydides.notes: Author name 'THUDYDIDES' is a clear typo for 'THUKYDIDES' (Thucydides). Corrected in display_name. Publication year given as 'o.J.' (ohne Jahr = no year), so publication_year is null.

STUDIENBUCH LINGUISTIK
Von Linke, Nussbaumer, Portmann. 2. Auflage, ergänzt um ein Kapitel 'Phonetik und Phonologie' von Urs Willi. Tübingen Niemeyer 1994. 472 S. OBrosch.

Portmann — but since the entry does not provide given names, missing_person is flagged. Urs Willi is listed as contributor for the added chapter on Phonetik und Phonologie.

STUDIENBUCH LINGUISTIK
Von Linke, Nussbaumer, Portmann. 2. Auflage, ergänzt um ein Kapitel 'Phonetik und Phonologie' von Urs Willi. Tübingen Niemeyer 1994. 472 S. OBrosch.

Portmann — but since the entry does not provide given names, missing_person is flagged. Urs Willi is listed as contributor for the added chapter on Phonetik und Phonologie.

Von Linke, Nussbaumer, Portmann. 2. Auflage, ergänzt um ein Kapitel 'Phonetik und Phonologie' von Urs Willi. Tübingen Niemeyer 1994. 472 S. OBrosch.

Portmann — but since the entry does not provide given names, missing_person is flagged. Urs Willi is listed as contributor for the added chapter on Phonetik und Phonologie.

scheibe.original_entry:
GEORG KOLBE IB 422
Bildwerke. Vom Künstler ausgewählt. 28 Tafeln. Nachwort Richard Graul Leipzig 1939. 56 S. Einband berieben.2. Ex. Nachwort von Scheibe.
scheibe.notes: The entry mentions two different authors of the Nachwort: first 'Richard Graul', then a second copy ('2. Ex.') with 'Nachwort von Scheibe'. This suggests two copies may have different editions or printings with different afterword authors. Both contributors are listed. It is unclear whether the second copy is a different edition or the same edition with a different contributor noted in error. Human verification recommended.

lamartine.original_entry:
LAMARTINE
Girondisten und Jakobiner. In achtunddreißig Porträts mit den vierzig Stichen von August Raffet herausgegeben und übertragen von Alfred Neumann. München Desch (1946). 261 S. OPbd. m. OU, dieser mit kleinen Einrissen, sonst sehr gut erhalten.
lamartine.notes: Alfred Neumann is listed both as editor ('herausgegeben') and translator ('übertragen'). The original work by Lamartine is likely 'Histoire des Girondins' (1847). The full given name of LAMARTINE (Alphonse de Lamartine) is not provided in the entry.

tissot.original_entry:
TISSOT
Von der Gesundheit der Gelehrten. A. d. Französ. Zürich Füßli. 1768. 12, 263 S. OPbd. in Schuber. Neuwertiger Reprintband.
tissot.notes: The entry lists '12, 263 S.' — the '12' likely refers to 12 pages of prefatory material (Roman-numerated pages), with 263 as the main text pages; only 263 captured in pages field. The entry describes a 'Reprintband' (reprint), yet the publication year given is 1768, which would be the original publication date. The actual reprint year is not stated. TISSOT's given name is not provided; the author is likely Samuel Auguste André David Tissot (1728–1797), Swiss physician, whose work 'De la santé des gens de lettres' (1768) matches this title — flagged for verification.

holub.original_entry:
HOLUB
Sieben Jahre in Süd-Afrika. Wien Hölder 1881. OLn. fehlt Band 1.
holub.notes: The entry notes 'fehlt Band 1', indicating this is a multi-volume work with Band 1 missing. The total number of volumes is not stated. The author 'HOLUB' appears to be Emil Holub, the Czech-Austrian explorer and physician known for his travels in southern Africa; the full given name is absent from the entry. Volume structure cannot be fully parsed due to missing volume data.

goldhagen.original_entry:
HERODOTUS
Neun Bücher der Geschichte. Nach der Übersetzung von Goldhagen vollständig herausgegeben München Georg Müller 1911. 408, 441 SS. Buchausstattung von Paul Renner. Originalhalblederbände mit Lesebändchen. Sehr schöne Exemplare.

rölleke.original_entry:
BRÜDER GRIMM
Wiegen- und Kinderlieder gesammelt durch die…Hrsg.u.komm.von Rölleke. Weimar Böhlau 1999. 160 S. mit Illustrationen. OPbd.m.OU. Erstveröffentlichung. Ungelesen
rölleke.notes: The title is truncated with '…' suggesting the full title is incomplete. The editor's first name is missing — only 'Rölleke' is given; likely Heinz Rölleke, a well-known Grimm scholar, but the first name is not stated in the entry, so missing_person is set true.

eugippius.original_entry:
EUGIPPIUS
Das Leben des heiligen Severin. Lateinisch und Deutsch. Einführung, Übersetzung und Erläuterungen von Rudolf Noll. Mit 1 Karte. Berlin Akademie-Verlag 1963. VIII, 150 S. OLn. m. OU. Mit einer Widmung von Noll.

jamblich.original_entry:
JAMBLICH
Pythagoras: Legende – Lehre – Lebensgestaltung. Eingeleitet, übersetzt von…Darmstadt Wiss. Buchgesellschaft 2002. 352 S. OPbd.
jamblich.notes:
mvc: Eingeleitet, übersetzt und mit interpretierenden Essays versehen von Michael von Albrecht, John Dillon, Martin George, Michael Lurje, und David S. du Toit.
 The entry states 'Eingeleitet, übersetzt von…' but the name of the introducer/translator is missing (indicated by the ellipsis). missing_person set to true. is_translation set to true since a translator is indicated even though unnamed.

vermeer.original_entry:
VERMEER
Das Gesamtwerk. Herausgeber Arthur K Wheelock, Jr. Katalog: Ben Broos und Arthur K. Wheelock, Jr. Mit Beiträgen von : Albert Blankert und Jörgen Wadum . Stuttgart Belser 1992, 2. durchges. Auflage. Ausstellungskatalog Königliche Gemäldegalerie Mauritshuis Den Haag 1996. Folio 229 S. Mit zahlr. Abb. Verlagsfrisch
vermeer.notes: multiple_editions: The entry describes a Stuttgart Belser 1992 edition (2. durchgesehene Auflage) as the main record, and also references an exhibition catalogue at the Mauritshuis Den Haag 1996, which appears to be a separate or related edition/publication. The main record fields reflect the first-described Stuttgart/Belser 1992 edition. api_concerned: The entry lists VERMEER as the author, but Vermeer is the subject of the catalogue, not its author. The actual editors and catalogue authors are Wheelock, Broos, Blankert, and Wadum. The author field has been populated as given in the entry heading, but human review is recommended to reassign authorship correctly. Additionally, the relationship between the 1992 Belser publication and the 1996 Mauritshuis exhibition catalogue is ambiguous — they may be the same work in different editions or two distinct publications.

mrazek.original_entry:
ENTWICKLUNG DER WIENER SCHULE
Künstlerhaus Wien Ausstellung 12. Juni- 21. Juli 1968. Mit Beiträgen von Gütersloh, Mrazek, Kubin und Kokoschka. Signaturen von einigen Künstlern.
mrazek.notes: This appears to be an exhibition catalogue for the Künstlerhaus Wien, dated 12 June – 21 July 1968. The contributors are listed only by surname (Gütersloh, Mrazek, Kubin, Kokoschka); full names could not be confirmed unambiguously from the entry alone. 'Gütersloh' likely refers to Albert Paris Gütersloh, 'Kubin' to Alfred Kubin, 'Kokoschka' to Oskar Kokoschka, and 'Mrazek' possibly to Wilhelm Mrazek — human verification recommended. No publisher name beyond 'Künstlerhaus Wien' is given; place of publication inferred as Wien.

schwarzenberg.original_entry:
SCHWARZENBERG
Briefe des Feldmarschalls Fürsten Schwarzenberg an seine Frau 1799 – 1816. Herausgegeben von Johann Friedrich Novak. Wien Gerlach & Wiedling 1913. Mit 2 farbigen Porträts. 461 OBrosch. Schönes, unaufgeschnittenes Exemplar. 2. Ex. A`
schwarzenberg.notes: The author field lists only 'SCHWARZENBERG' without a given name. The Feldmarschall Fürst Schwarzenberg referred to here is Karl Philipp zu Schwarzenberg (1771–1820), commander at the Battle of Leipzig. The entry is consistent with this identification, but the given name is absent from the cataloging entry. The trailing 'A`' at the end of the entry is unexplained and may be a shelf mark or cataloging notation.

holbach.original_entry:
HOLBACH, Du Marsais
Essay über die Vorurteile. A.d.Französ, Leipzig Reclam 1972. 438 S. TB.
holbach.notes: The entry lists two authors: HOLBACH and Du Marsais. The 'Essai sur les préjugés' (1770) is a work attributed to Paul-Henri Thiry d'Holbach, but it was also attributed to César Chesneau Du Marsais in some editions. The dual authorship listing is historically plausible but ambiguous — it is unclear whether both are intended as co-authors or whether one is the subject and the other the actual author. Human verification recommended.

deutschmeisterbund.original_entry:
HOEN, Max
Die Deutschmeister. Taten und Schicksale des Infanterieregiments Hoch- und Deutschmeister Nr. 4 insbesondere im Weltkriege. Verfasst von Feldmarschalleutnant Max Hoen, Oberst Josef Waldstätten-Zipperer und Major Josef Seifert. Mit 5 farbigen Tafeln, 172 Illustrationen und 56 Kartenskizzen. Herausgegeben vom Deutschmeisterbunde. Wien Österreichische Staatsdruckerei 1928. OLn. m. Lesebändchen. Sehr gut erhalten.

kaufmann.original_entry:
KAUFMANN,
Hans Erich Apostel. Eine Studie von …Österreichische Komponisten des XX. Jahrhunderts Band 4. Wien Österreichischer Bundesverlag o.J. 79 S. Mit zahlr. Abb. OPbd. 2. Ex.
kaufmann.notes: missing_person: The author's given name appears to be missing — the entry reads 'KAUFMANN,' followed by a line break, then 'Hans Erich Apostel' which is the subject's name, not the author's. The phrase 'Eine Studie von …' indicates an author should follow but the name is absent. api_concerned: The entry lists 'Band 4' of the series 'Österreichische Komponisten des XX. Jahrhunderts', but the series volume number has not been captured in the volumes array since this does not appear to be a multi-volume work per se but rather a series entry. Additionally, the author's identity cannot be confirmed without the missing given name.

vollmer.original_entry:
VOLLMER
Wörterbuch der Mythologie aller Völker. Neu bearbeitet von W. Binder. Mit einer Einführung in die mythologische Wissenschaft von Johannes Minchwitz. Suttgart Hoffmann 1874. 3. Aufl. LXX, 456 SS. In Doppelspalten und 303 Illustrationen. OHLn. Reprint
vollmer.notes:
mvc: One note: the entry also has Johannes Minchwitz — the correct spelling is Johannes Minckwitz (with a 'k'). 
Typo corrected: 'Suttgart' → 'Stuttgart'. The entry lists LXX (70) preliminary pages and 456 main pages in double columns; pages field left null as the total is ambiguous. 'Reprint' noted in condition field as it describes the physical copy's nature.

palaiphatos.original_entry:
PALAIPHATOS
Unglaubliche Geschichten. Die Wahrheit über die griechischen Mythen. Griechisch-Deutsch übersetzt und herausgegeben von Kai Brodersen. Stuttgart Reclam 2002. 149 S. Reclams Universal-Bibliothek 18200. TB.

dehio.original_entry:
DEHIO
WIEN, NIEDERÖSTERREICH, OBERÖSTERREICH UND BURGENLAND
Herausgegeben von Dagobert Frey und Karl Ginhart unter Mitwirkung von Richard Kurt Donin, Erwin Hainisch, Eberhard Hempel, Justus Schmidt und Hans Tietze. Mit zahlr. Faltplänen und Plänen im Text. Wien, Berlin Schroll und Deutscher Kunstverlag 1935. XII, 704 S. OLn. Dehio Handbuch der deutschen Kunstdenkmäler Österreich II. Sehr gut erhalten.

gasser.original_entry:
ÖSTERREICHISCHE GESCHICHTE Hrsg. Von Herwig Wolfram
Fehlt
AM RANDE DES REICHES.
Die Römer in Österreich 15. V. Chr. – 388 n. Chr.
Von Gasser, Jilek, Wolfram
gasser.notes:
mvc: Typo bei Gassner. The original entry lists Gasser, Jilek, Wolfram as authors. The actual authors of the volume are Verena Gassner, Sonja Jilek, and Sabine Ladstätter — not Wolfram. Herwig Wolfram is the editor of the series, not a co-author of this volume. The handwritten/typed entry seems to have substituted Wolfram for Ladstätter. The end date in the entry is 388 n. Chr. but the actual published volume covers up to 378 n. Chr.  
The entry is marked 'Fehlt' (missing/wanted), indicating this is a search/want list entry for a copy not currently held. The authors are listed only by surname (Gasser, Jilek, Wolfram) without given names; full names cannot be determined with certainty from the entry alone. This volume is part of the multi-volume series 'Österreichische Geschichte' edited by Herwig Wolfram; the subtitle 'Am Rande des Reiches. Die Römer in Österreich 15. V. Chr. – 388 n. Chr.' identifies it as a specific volume in that series. Based on world knowledge, the authors are likely Verena Gassner, Sonja Jilek, and Herwig Wolfram, but this cannot be confirmed from the entry text alone, so api_concerned is set. missing_person is set because given names are absent for all three authors.

Based on world knowledge, the authors are likely Verena Gassner, Sonja Jilek, and Herwig Wolfram, but this cannot be confirmed from the entry text alone, so api_concerned is set. missing_person is set because given names are absent for all three authors.

czezik-müller.original_entry:
CZEZIK-MÜLLER
Österreichs Post. Einst und jetzt. Eine Sammlung von Bildern aus der österreichischen Postgeschichte. Mit erläuterndem Text von … Anhang: Überblick über die 'Telegraphie und Telephonie in Österreich' von Hans Pfeuffer. Mit zahlr. z.T. farbigen Abb. Herausgegeben im Einvernehmen mit der Generaldirektion für die Post- und Telegraphenverwaltung in Österreich. Wien um 1927. 131 S. Quer-4°. Mit einer 6 zeiligen Widmung des Generaldirektors Hoheisel. Bunter OLn. Band. Sehr gut erhalten.
czezik-müller.notes: missing_person: The entry contains 'Mit erläuterndem Text von …' with an ellipsis indicating the author's name is missing or incomplete. CZEZIK-MÜLLER is listed as the primary author but may be the editor or compiler; the exact role is unclear. The publication year is given as 'um 1927' (approximately 1927); 1927 has been used as the best estimate.

abaelard.original_entry:
ABAELARD
Die Leidensgeschichte und der Briefwechsel mit Heloisa. Berlin Union Verlag 1963. 94 S. Oln.m.OU u. Lesebändchen.
2. Ex. Originalausgabe Lambert Schneider 3. Erw. Aufl. OLn.m.OU, Lesebändchen und Kassette. Sehr gut erhalten. 2 Ex.
abaelard.notes: corrected_by_api: 'Oln.' corrected to 'OLn.' (standard abbreviation for Originalleinen). multiple_editions: The main record is the Berlin Union Verlag 1963 edition (94 S., 2 copies). A second edition is also described: Originalausgabe Lambert Schneider, 3. erweiterte Auflage, OLn.m.OU, Lesebändchen und Kassette, sehr gut erhalten, 2 Ex. The copy count '2. Ex.' at the start of the second description appears to be a separator notation rather than a copy count for the first edition; the first edition's copy count is taken as 2 from the '2. Ex.' marker, but this is ambiguous — it may mean the second edition entry begins here. The second edition details are noted here only and not merged into the main record fields.

Einführung in die neuere deutsche Literaturwissenschaft. Ein Arbeitsbuch. 8.neu-bearbeitete Auflage. Berli Erich Schmidt Verlag 2009. 289 S. OBrosch.
wagner-egelhaaf.notes: Place of publication 'Berli' corrected to 'Berlin' (obvious typo). The second author 'WAGNER-EGELHAAF' appears without a given name; the full name is likely 'WAGNER-EGELHAAF, Martina', but this has not been inserted as it cannot be confirmed from the entry alone — missing_person flag set.

meldemann.original_entry:
MELDEMANN
Holzschnitt von der Belagerung Wiens durch die Türken im Jahre 1929. Schönes Faksimile mit Beschreibung aller Szenen. Mehrfach gefalteter Plan in HLn.- Bindung.
meldemann.notes: The year '1929' in the title was corrected to '1529': the Ottoman siege of Vienna took place in 1529, not 1929. This is an unambiguous date error. The entry refers to the famous panoramic woodcut map of Vienna by Niklas Meldemann (published 1530), depicting the 1529 siege.

stadt mödling.original_entry:
WILDGANS, Anton
In Memoriam Anton Wildgans 1881 – 1956. Herausgegeben von der Stadt Mödling (1956). OPbd. 63 S. Sehr gut.
stadt mödling.notes: Anton Wildgans (1881–1932) was an Austrian poet and playwright who died in 1932, not 1956. The date range '1881 – 1956' in the title likely refers to the publication year 1956 being the 75th anniversary of his birth, not his death year. The title has been preserved as written. The year 1956 in the title is retained verbatim as it appears to be part of the commemorative title rather than a biographical death date. No correction applied to the title itself, but this discrepancy is noted.

Supplemente 1,2 und 3.
Supplemente 4,5 und 6.

Comische Erzählungen Combabus. Der verklagte Amor. 1. Aufl.1984. Mit gestochenem Frontispiz und Nr. 5692. als Band XVI doppelt. Zusammen ca. 6400 Seiten. Verlagsneue Originalleinenbände. Bd. XVI OHldr.
hamburger stiftung zur förderung von wis.notes: The volume structure is complex and partially ambiguous. The entry lists 16 line-break-separated content blocks but the mapping of content blocks to volume numbers is not explicitly stated — the assignment here is inferred from the sequence. Band XV is explicitly identified as 'C. M. Wielands Leben von J. G. Gruber', and Band XVI as 'Comische Erzählungen Combabus. Der verklagte Amor.' The phrase 'als Band XVI doppelt' is unclear — it may mean a duplicate copy of Band XVI exists, or that Band XVI is counted twice in the set. The publisher name is not explicitly stated in the entry (only the place 'Hamburg' and year 1984 are given); the publisher is likely 'Hamburger Stiftung zur Förderung von Wissenschaft und Kultur' or a commercial imprint, but cannot be confirmed from the entry alone. The total page count of 'ca. 6400 Seiten' is approximate. The note 'Nr. 5692' suggests a numbered copy of Band XVI specifically.

danz, d.j.t.l..notes: Only volumes 3 and 4 are present in the entry; volumes 1 and 2 are explicitly noted as missing ('Fehlen Band 1 und 2'). The role of DANZ, D.J.T.L. is ambiguous — the entry lists them as the primary name but does not explicitly state 'Übersetzt von'; it is assumed they are the translator/editor of this bilingual Latin-German edition of Plautus. The actual author of the works is Plautus (Titus Maccius Plautus); the entry uses 'Marcus Accius Plautus' which is a variant name form. api_concerned is set because the name 'Marcus Accius Plautus' differs from the standard 'Titus Maccius Plautus', and the role of DANZ is not explicitly stated.

klaar, alfred.original_entry:
BÖRNE, Ludwig
Gesammelte Schriften. Vollständige Ausgabe in sechs Bänden. Nachgelassene Schriften in zwei Bänden. Mit einer biographisch-kritischer Einleitung von Alfred Klaar. Leipzig Hesse 8 Teile in drei Bänden.
1. Band:
Teil 1. Biographie. Erzählungen Reisen Vermischte Schriften. XCV, 400 S.
Teil 2. Schilderungen aus Paris. Aus meinem Tagebuch. Dramatische Blätter. 442 S.
Teil 4. Briefe. Menzel. Fragmente und Französische Aufsätze. 331 S.
2. Band:
Teil 3. Nachträge zu den dramatischen Blättern. Kritiken. 251 S.
Teil 5. Briefe aus Paris. 328 S.
3. Band.:
Teil 6. Briefe aus Paris Fortsetzung. 328 S.
Teil 7. Nachgelassene Schriften I. 160 S.
Teil 8. Nachgelassene Schriften II. u. a. 222 S.
Originalleinenbände. Sehr gut erhalten
klaar, alfred.notes: The entry describes 8 Teile (parts) distributed across 3 physical Bände (volumes), but the numbering of parts within Band 1 is non-sequential: Teil 1, Teil 2, and Teil 4 are listed under Band 1, while Teil 3 appears under Band 2. Teil 3 (251 S.) precedes Teil 5 in Band 2, suggesting the parts were not bound in strict numerical order. This irregular distribution cannot be resolved without physical inspection of the volumes. No publication year is given. The subtitle states 'sechs Bänden' for the main work and 'zwei Bänden' for the Nachgelassene Schriften, but the physical binding is in three volumes containing eight parts — this structural discrepancy is noted.

loos, erich.original_entry:
CASANOVA, Giacomo Chevalier de Seingalt
GESCHICHTE MEINES LEBENS. Mit einem Essay von Peter Quennell. Herausgegeben und eingeleitet von Erich Loos. Erstmals nach der Urfassung ins Deutsche übersetzt von Heinz Sauter.
Band I bis III. 403, 357, 392 S.
Band IV bis VI. 390, 348, 340 S.
Band VII bis IX. Mit einem Essay von Friedrich Georg Jünger. 389, 337, 472 S.
Band X bis XII. 416, 409, 411 S.
EDUARD UND ELISABETH oder die Reise in das Innere unseres Erdballs. Roman.
Band 1 bis III. 428, 386, 376 S.
VERMISCHTE SCHRIFTEN. GESAMMELTE BRIEFE
Ausgewählt, eingeleitet und mit Anmerkungen versehen von Enrico Straub. Nach den französischen, italienischen und lateinischen Originalhandschriften oder Erstausgaben neu übersetzt von Heinz von Sauter und Enrico Straub. 569, 414, 581 S.
DIE BILDER AUS
Geschichte meines Lebens. Vermischte Schriften. Gesammelte Briefe. 307 S.
In 7 Bänden. Frankfurt Zweitausendeins 1980. Lizenzausgabe vom Propyläenverlag. Originalleinenbände mit Lesebändchen. Alle neuwertig.
loos, erich.notes: The entry describes a complex multi-volume set published in 7 physical volumes (Bände), but the internal structure groups multiple sub-volumes together (e.g., Band I–III of 'Geschichte meines Lebens' in one physical volume). The mapping of the 12 sub-volumes of 'Geschichte meines Lebens' plus 'Eduard und Elisabeth' (3 sub-volumes), 'Vermischte Schriften/Gesammelte Briefe' (3 sub-volumes), and the picture volume (1 volume) to exactly 7 physical volumes is ambiguous and cannot be cleanly resolved. The translator is named 'Heinz Sauter' in one place and 'Heinz von Sauter' in another; 'Heinz von Sauter' has been used as the canonical form since it appears in the more detailed credit. The entry also covers multiple distinct works by Casanova (Geschichte meines Lebens, Eduard und Elisabeth, Vermischte Schriften, Gesammelte Briefe, and a picture volume), making the main title assignment to 'Geschichte meines Lebens' a simplification. Human verification recommended.

gsell-fels, th..original_entry:
GSELL-FELS Th.
Ober-Italien. Zweite umgearbeitete und vermehrte Auflage. Mit 10 Karten, 38 Plänen und Grundrissen, 20 Ansichten in Stahlstich, 1 Panorama und 64 Ansichten in Holschnitt. Leipzig Bibliograph. Institut 1875. 1455 S. doppelspaltig, 72 S. Annoncen-Beilage. Meyers Reisebücher. Zwei Bände von Meyers Reisen in einem Band. Solide, den Reisebüchern angepasste Bindung der Zeit ohne Aufdruck. Inhaltlich kaum Benützungsspuren erkennbar. Die 20 Stahlstiche tadellos! Annoncen-Beilage 72 S.2. Ex. 1874. OLn. Vollständig. Annoncen-Beilage 44 S. Sehr gut erhalten.
gsell-fels, th..notes: multiple_editions: Two copies are described. The first (main record) is the 1875 second edition, 1455 S. doppelspaltig with 72 S. Annoncen-Beilage, in a period binding without imprint. The second copy ('2. Ex.') is dated 1874, in OLn., with a 44 S. Annoncen-Beilage, described as very well preserved. The year difference (1875 vs. 1874) and differing Annoncen-Beilage page counts suggest these may be different printings or editions. The first copy is treated as the main record per cataloging convention. problematic_multi_volume: The entry states 'Zwei Bände von Meyers Reisen in einem Band', meaning two volumes bound as one physical book. The volume structure is ambiguous — it is unclear whether the two volumes have separate titles or page counts. The volumes array reflects the two physical copies rather than two distinct textual volumes, as no separate volume titles or page counts are given.

herold, heinrich.notes: This is a youth adaptation of Harriet Beecher Stowe's 'Uncle Tom's Cabin', rewritten by Heinrich Herold — not a translation per se, but a retelling ('für die Jugend geschrieben'). The original work is by Beecher Stowe, and Herold is the adapter. The entry lists Herold as the author, which is consistent with the cataloging convention for adaptations. is_translation is set to false since this is an adaptation rather than a direct translation. The subtitle note 'Nach Beecher-Stowe' clarifies the source. Flagged for human review to confirm authorship attribution is handled correctly.

Reisen in die Aequinoctial-Gegenden des neuen Continents. In deutscher Bearbeitung von Hermann Hauff. Nach der Anmerkung und unter Mitwirkung des Verfassers. Einzige von A. v. Humboldt anerkannte Ausgabe in deutscher Sprache. Stuttgart Cotta 1861-1862. Drei Teile in einem Band. Band 1: XVI, 260; 300; 242 S. OHLdr. m. goldgeprägtem Rücken. Sehr gut erhalten. Gr.8°. hauff, hermann.notes: The work was originally written in French ('Voyage aux régions équinoxiales du Nouveau Continent'). Hermann Hauff is listed both as contributor (German adaptation) and effectively as translator; both roles are noted. The entry covers Band 1 (containing Teile 1-3 in one physical volume, with pages XVI+260, 300, and 242) and notes that Band 2 (Teile 4-6) is missing from the collection. Because Band 2 is absent, its volume data cannot be parsed. The publication year spans 1861-1862; 1861 is used as the main year. The total_volumes cannot be determined with certainty from the entry alone. The role of 'Hauff' is ambiguous: described as 'deutsche Bearbeitung' (adaptation/reworking) rather than a straight translation, though the work is clearly a translation from French; api_concerned is set accordingly.

Vierter Band. Malerische Länder- und Völkerkunde. Fünfte hie und da veränderte Auflage mit 120 Abbildungen. 1864. XVI, 784 S.
Erschienen bei Hempel, Berlin. In drei Bänden. OHLn. mit Rückenvergoldung. Innen gut, sonst mäßig erhalten.
zimmermann, w.f.a..notes: The entry states 'In drei Bänden' (in three volumes) at the end, but the content describes four numbered volumes (Erster, Zweiter, Dritter, Vierter Band), making the volume count contradictory. The entry also notes that 'Dritter Band erste Abtheilung fehlt' (Third volume, first part is missing), which may explain the discrepancy — the physical set as held may consist of three bound volumes despite the work being published in four numbered volumes. Each volume has a different edition number (15th, 16th, 15th, 5th), indicating multiple editions across volumes; this is flagged under multiple_editions. The missing first Abtheilung of Band 3 is noted in the volume entry for Band 3. total_volumes is set to 4 per the textual content, not the 'In drei Bänden' note.

delitzsch, friedrich.notes: The entry describes two separate works by the same author bound together in one volume. The first (main) entry is 'Babel und Bibel. Ein Vortrag', Leipzig, Hinrichssche Buchhandlung, 1903, 78 S., 46.–50. Tausend, Dritte durchgesehene Ausgabe, with 51 illustrations. The second work bound with it is 'Zweiter Vortrag über Babel und Bibel', Stuttgart, Deutsche Verlags-Anstalt, 1903, 50 S., 36.–40. Tausend, with 17 illustrations in text and 3 coloured plates. Both are physically bound together as one volume (OHLdr. mit goldgeprägtem Rücken). The multiple_editions flag is set because two distinct editions/works with different publishers, page counts, and edition statements are described together. Alt-Wiener Porträts. Lebensbilder mit einer kulturgeschichtlichen Schilderung Wiens während des 19. Jahrhunderts. Mit 40 Bildern. Graz Verlagsbuchhandlung 'Styria' 1927. 247 S. OLn.m.Gold- und Blindprägung. Sehr gut erhalten.

kisch, wilhelm.original_entry:
KISCH, Wilhelm
Die alten Strassen und Plaetze von Wien´s Vorstädten und ihre historisch interessanten Häuser. Ein Beitrag zur Culturgeschichte Wien`s mit Rücksicht auf vaterländische Kunst, Architektur, Musik und Literatur. I. Band. Mit 201 Illustrationen. Wien Oskar Frank 1888. (4), IV, 576 S. Bunter OLn. Band mit schönen Vorsätzen und 3 fachem Goldschnitt. Sehr gut erhalten.
Band 2.: Die alten Strassen und Plätze Wiens und seine historisch interessantesten Häuser. Ein Beitrag zur Culturgschichte Wiens mit Rücksicht auf Vaterländische Kunst, Architektur, Musik, Literatur etc. In 20 Heften mit zahlreichen Illustrationen. Wien Selbstverlag des Verfassers 1879. XXXII S. Anschließend noch einmal das Titelblatt. Zusätzlich dazu: Mit einem Plane und 277 Illustrationen nach den Originalen von… Wien M. Gottlieb 1883. OLn. Band, sowie Band 1, ohne Goldschnitt. Einband vom Buchblock gelöst, durch Leinenstreifen leicht zu reparieren. Sonst neuwertig. Zus.
kisch, wilhelm.notes: missing_person: In Band 2, the illustrator/original artist is referenced with 'nach den Originalen von…' but the name is cut off with an ellipsis — the person is absent. multiple_editions: Band 2 appears to have two distinct publication records: one from Wien Selbstverlag des Verfassers 1879 and one from Wien M. Gottlieb 1883, which may represent different editions or parts of the same volume; these cannot be cleanly separated. problematic_multi_volume: The structure of Band 2 is ambiguous — it is unclear whether the 1879 and 1883 publications are sub-volumes, separate editions, or supplementary material. The top-level fields reflect Band 1 (the first-described volume) as the main record. api_concerned: The title and subtitle differ between Band 1 and Band 2 (Band 1 refers to 'Vorstädten', Band 2 to 'Wiens' more broadly), and the publishers and years differ significantly (1888 vs. 1879/1883), suggesting these may not be a straightforward two-volume set but rather related but distinct publications. Human verification recommended.

ATLAS MAIOR

Germania 215 S.
Germania Austria Helvetia 223-404 S.

bläu, johannes.notes: corrected_by_api: The author name 'Bläu' is likely a typo for 'Blaeu' (Joan Blaeu, the Dutch cartographer responsible for the Atlas Maior of 1665). The Taschen 2006 reprint is well documented. Correction applied conservatively as 'Bläu' is the form given in the entry and 'Blaeu' is the standard form. api_concerned: The Taschen 2006 edition of the Atlas Maior is known to exist in multiple thematic volumes; the two volumes described here ('Germania' and 'Germania Austria Helvetia') appear to be the same or overlapping volume. The first volume is titled 'Germania' with 215 pages, and the second is 'Germania Austria Helvetia' with pages 223-404 (182 pages in continuous pagination). This continuous pagination suggests these may be two parts of a single volume rather than two independent volumes, or the entry may reflect a specific two-volume subset of the larger Taschen series. Human verification recommended.

hancarville, pierre-francois hugues.original_entry:
HANCARVILLE, Pierre-Francois Hugues
Bilder aus dem Privatleben der römischen Cäsaren. Und Denkmäler des Geheimkults der römischen Damen. A.d.Französ. von Heinrich Conradt und Alfred Semerau. 2 Bände. Mit 50 und 48 Abb. Desch (Basel) ca. 1965. 310, 212 S. Sonderausgabe für Wissenschaftler… Verpflichtungserklärung liegt bei. Nummerierte Ausgabe mit der Nr. 3947. Original Seiden- Bände in Seidenschuber. Sehr gut erhalten.
hancarville, pierre-francois hugues.notes: corrected_by_api: Publication year given as 'ca. 1965' — recorded as 1965 with the approximation noted. The hyphenated 'Seiden- Bände' in the original appears to be a line-break artifact; expanded to 'Seidenbände'. api_concerned: The author Pierre-François Hugues d'Hancarville (1719–1805) was an 18th-century antiquarian; the works described here appear to be erotic antiquarian publications. The translator names 'Heinrich Conradt' and 'Alfred Semerau' are listed together; both are known translators of erotic/historical literature, which is consistent. The publisher 'Desch' in Basel is plausible for this type of Sonderausgabe. The 'Verpflichtungserklärung liegt bei' indicates this was a restricted scholarly edition. No major identity concerns, but the approximate date and the unusual edition type warrant human verification.

However, the entry structure is ambiguous: 'HAUSNER' appears as the cataloged author, yet 'Rudolf Hausner' also appears among the contributors providing text. It is possible that 'HAUSNER' as the main entry refers to Rudolf Hausner as the subject/artist of the monograph, with Bockelmann providing photos and Hacker, Hausner, and Hocke providing texts. schwend, regni maria.original_entry:
ELIO SCHMITZ / ITALO SVEVO
Meine alte, unglückliche Familie Schmitz. Elios Tagebuch und andere Zeugnisse. Herausgegeben vun aus dem Italienischen überrsetzt von Regni Maria Schwend. Wien Zsolnay 1999. 239 S. Opbd.
schwend, regni maria.notes: Typos corrected: 'vun' → 'von', 'überrsetzt' → 'übersetzt'. The entry states 'Herausgegeben vun aus dem Italienischen überrsetzt von Regni Maria Schwend', which conflates editor and translator roles into one person; Schwend is listed as both editor and translator. The author field is ambiguous: the entry header lists both 'ELIO SCHMITZ' and 'ITALO SVEVO' — Italo Svevo was the pen name of Ettore Schmitz, and Elio Schmitz was his son; it is unclear whether both are authors or whether one is the subject. The format notation 'Opbd.' is interpreted as 'Originalpappband' (OPbd.), though the lowercase 'p' may be a minor variant. Human verification recommended for author attribution and dual editor/translator role.

humboldt, a. v..original_entry:
REISE in die Aequinoctialgegenden des neuen Continents.
In deutscher Bearbeitung von Hermann Hauff. Nach der Anordnung und unter Mitwirkung des Verfassers. Einzige von A. v. Humboldt anerkannte Ausgabe in deutscher Sprache. Stuttgart Cotta 1861-62. Band 1 bis 3 von sechs Teilen. XVI, 260; 300; 242 S. OHLdr. m. goldgepr. Rücken. Sehr gut erhalten. Band 2 fehlt.
humboldt, a. v..notes: The entry states 'Band 1 bis 3 von sechs Teilen' (Bands 1 to 3 of six parts), with three page counts (XVI, 260; 300; 242 S.) and notes that 'Band 2 fehlt' (Band 2 is missing). This is ambiguous: if Band 2 is missing, only two physical volumes are present, yet three page counts are given. It is unclear whether the page counts correspond to Bands 1, 3, and possibly another band, or whether the numbering refers to 'Teile' (parts) rather than physical volumes. The volumes array has been populated conservatively: Band 1 (XVI+260 S.), and the two remaining counts assigned to Bands 3 and 4 tentatively, but this assignment is uncertain. Human verification is required. Additionally, the work is Alexander von Humboldt's 'Relation historique du Voyage aux régions équinoxiales du Nouveau Continent', originally in French; the author has been inferred from context ('A. v. Humboldt anerkannte Ausgabe') as the entry does not list an author explicitly.

nissing, hanns-gregor.notes: The entry covers two volumes (Band 1 and Band 3) of the series 'Einführende Schriften', implying at least a Band 2 exists that is not described here. borhek, august christian.original_entry:
XENOPHONS
Sämtliche Schriften. Aus dem Griechischen neu übersetzt von August Christian Borhek.
Erster Theil, welcher die Kyropädie mit den vornehmsten Abhandlungen über selbige enthält. Dazu Anhang ,welcher einige der besten Erläuterungsschriften der Kyropädie enthält. Mit einem Titelkupfer und Vignette. XVI, 535 S.
Zweyter Theil, welcher den Feldzug des Prinzen Kyros, mit den besten Erläuterungsschriften desselben enthält. Dazu Thomas Hutchinsons Abhandlung , vom Verfasser des Feldzugs des Prinzen Kyros, und vom Zuge der Griechen. Im Auszuge. Mit einem Titelkupfer und Vignette VI, 413 S.
Dritter Theil, welcher die griechische Geschichte enthält und einer Widmung des Übersetzers Borheck von 1789. Mit einem Titelkupfer und Vignette (XVIII), 509 S
Vierter Theil. Erster Anhang zur griechischen Geschichte Xenophons, oder Einleitung in dieselbe. Dazu Xenophons Lobschrift auf den König Agesilaus, übersetzt von Konrad Borheck. Weiters Xenophons Staatsverfassung der Lakedämonier. Übersestzt von Konrad Borheck. Weiters Xenophon über die Einkünfte, Xenophons Hiero und Xenophons Briefe übersetzt von Konrad Borheck. Mit einem Titelkupfer und Vignette. 464 S.
Wien und Prag bey Franz Haas 1801. Original grüne Pappbände mit Rückentitel, stark beansprucht. Buchblock, Text und Kupfer tadellos erhalten. Der fünfte Band fehlt. Seltenes Werk. Band 1 – 4 von 5
borhek, august christian.notes: 1) CORRECTED: The entry heading reads 'XENOPHONS' (genitive form); normalized to 'XENOPHON' as the author name. 2) CORRECTED: The translator's name is spelled inconsistently throughout the entry as both 'Borhek' (in the main title line) and 'Borheck' (in volumes 3 and 4). The form 'Borheck' appears more frequently and is used for the named co-translator Konrad Borheck; however, the correct historical spelling of the main translator is 'Borheck' — corrected_by_api set true for this normalization, though api_concerned is also set because both spellings appear in the original and the correct form cannot be verified with certainty. 3) PROBLEMATIC MULTI-VOLUME: The entry explicitly states 'Band 1–4 von 5' and notes 'Der fünfte Band fehlt.' Volume 5 is absent and its content, page count, and title are unknown; it has been omitted from the volumes array. 4) API_CONCERNED: Konrad Borheck is listed as a separate translator for portions of volume 4 (Lobschrift auf Agesilaus, Staatsverfassung der Lakedämonier, Hiero, Briefe, Einkünfte), distinct from the main translator August Christian Borhek/Borheck. The relationship between the two translators (possibly father and son or relatives) cannot be confirmed without further research. 5) TYPO NOTED: 'Übersestzt' in the original entry is an obvious typo for 'Übersetzt'; not corrected in the original_entry field but noted here.

The life dates given (1862–1947) are noted; however, world knowledge indicates Franz Servaes was born in 1862 but died in 1947 — this aligns with the entry. The dedication recipient Adolf Donath was a known Austrian art critic and journalist, which is consistent. No title or publisher is associated with this autograph item. The 'title' field has been used descriptively to capture the nature of the object.

ziehrer, carl michael.notes: This entry describes a multi-item autograph/memorabilia convolute rather than a standard bibliographic item. The entry does not correspond to a single published work and cannot be fully represented in standard bibliographic fields. Human review is recommended to determine appropriate cataloging treatment.

abert, hermann.notes: The entry is catalogued under 'MOZART, W. A.' but the actual author of this work is Hermann Abert ('Von Hermann Abert'), who produced a revised and expanded edition of Otto Jahn's Mozart biography. The title field has been set to 'Mozart' and the author to Abert accordingly. The two main volumes have different edition numbers and years (Vol. 1: 9. Auflage 1978; Vol. 2: 8. Auflage 1975), which constitutes multiple editions within the same set; the publication_year at top level reflects the later volume (1978). The register volume (Vol. 3) was compiled by Erich Kapst (1966) and is listed as a contributor. corrected_by_api is set true because the catalogued heading 'MOZART, W. A.' was reinterpreted as the subject rather than the author, with Hermann Abert correctly identified as the author based on the entry text and world knowledge.

müller, franz karl.original_entry:
MÜLLER, Franz Karl
W.A.Mozart Gesamtkatalog seiner Werke 'Köchel-Verzeichnis'. Neubearbeitet und herausgegeben von … Wien Paul Kaltschmid 1951. 448 S. OLn. m. OU. Neuwertig.
müller, franz karl.notes: missing_person: The entry states 'Neubearbeitet und herausgegeben von …' with an ellipsis, indicating the editor's name is absent. MÜLLER, Franz Karl is listed as the heading but may be the subject or compiler rather than the editor of this new edition. api_concerned: The Köchel-Verzeichnis is a well-known catalogue of Mozart's works. The standard editions were edited by Alfred Einstein (3rd ed., 1937) and later by Franz Giegling, Alexander Weinmann, and Gerd Sievers (6th ed., 1964). A 1951 Wien edition by Paul Kaltschmid is not readily identifiable in standard bibliographic sources, and the role of MÜLLER, Franz Karl in relation to this work is unclear. Human verification is recommended.

silberstein, neil a..original_entry:
FINKELSTEIN, Israel/Neil A. SILBERSTEIN
Keine Posaunen vor Jericho. Die archäologische Wahrheit über die Bibel. München Beck 2003. A.d.Engl. Zweite Auflage. 381 S. Mit zahlr. Abb. OPbd.m.OU
silberstein, neil a..notes: The co-author's name is given as 'SILBERSTEIN, Neil A.' but the correct name is 'SILBERMAN, Neil Asher'. This is a clear typo/error in the entry. Corrected to 'SILBERMAN, Neil A.' — wait: the entry reads 'SILBERSTEIN', but the actual author is Neil Asher Silberman. However, since 'Silberstein' vs 'Silberman' could be considered ambiguous (two different surnames), this correction is flagged. Actually, given that the book 'Keine Posaunen vor Jericho' is definitively co-authored by Israel Finkelstein and Neil Asher Silberman, and 'SILBERSTEIN' is not a known author of this work, this is an unambiguous typo. Corrected display_name to 'SILBERMAN, Neil A.' in the authors array.

The format notation 'it' is unclear — it may refer to the Insel Taschenbuch series abbreviation, but this is uncertain and has been left unexpanded.

pelsaert, francois.original_entry:
PELSAERT, Francois
Der Untergang der 'Batavia' und andere Schiffsjournale und Originalberichte aus der großen Zeit der niederländischen Seefahrt im 17. und 18. Jahrhundert. Mit 56 zeitgenössischen Darstellungen, Faksimiles und Karten. Tübingen Erdmann 1976. 350 S. OLn.m.OU
pelsaert, francois.notes: François Pelsaert was a Dutch VOC merchant who wrote the journal of the Batavia shipwreck (1629). However, the entry title suggests this is an anthology of multiple ship journals and reports from the 17th and 18th century, which implies Pelsaert may be only one of several authors or contributors, or that this is an edited compilation where Pelsaert is listed as the primary/lead author. The actual editor(s) of this compilation may be missing from the entry. Human verification recommended to confirm whether Pelsaert is correctly listed as sole author or whether an editor should be recorded.

owlglaß, dr..notes: The translator is listed as 'Dr. Owlglaß', which is a pseudonym. The original language is not stated in the entry; Charles de Coster was a Belgian author who wrote in French, so the original language is likely French, but this is not explicitly confirmed in the entry and has not been inserted. The pseudonym 'Dr. Owlglaß' (also spelled 'Owlglass') was used by Hans Erich Blaich — flagged for human verification.

hanak, anton.notes: The entry lists Anton Hanak as the author, but this is a museum exhibition catalogue about the sculptor Anton Hanak (1875–1934). The actual author/compiler of the catalogue is likely the museum or Wilhelm Mrazek (who is mentioned as the author of an accompanying manuscript). Hanak himself, having died in 1934, could not have authored a 1969 catalogue. The entry structure suggests Hanak is the subject, not the author. Human verification is needed to determine the correct author attribution.

behncke, w..original_entry:
ILLUSTRIERTE GESCHICHTE DES KUNSTGEWERBES
Erster Band: Das Kunstgewerbe im Altertum, im Mittelalter und zur Zeit der Renaissance von W. Behncke, O. von Falke, E. Pernice und G. Swarzenski. VIII, 654 S.
Zweiter Band: Das Kunstgewerbe in Barock, Rokoko, Louis-XVI, Empire und neuester Zeit, im Gebiete des Islams und in Ostasien von E.W. Braun, M. Dreger und J. Folnesics O. Kümmel und G. Lehnert. VIII, 896 S.
Buchausstattung von Emil Orlik. Mit ca. 1130 Textabbildungen und etwa 200 farbigen Tafeln. Kopfgoldschnitt und Lesebändchen. Berlin Oldenbourg Verlag (1907) Goldgeprägte OPgt-Bände.
Bd1 mit kleinem Einriss am Rücken, Band 2 vorderer Deckel außen leicht aufgeplatzt, hinterer Deckel innen. Sonst beide Bände tadellos erhalten.
behncke, w..notes: The format abbreviation 'OPgt' is interpreted as Original-Pergament (original parchment/vellum binding), which is consistent with the description 'Goldgeprägte OPgt-Bände' for a prestige 1907 publication. However, 'OPgt' is not a standard RAK abbreviation and may stand for something else; flagged for verification. The publication year appears in parentheses (1907), suggesting it may be an estimated or approximate date as given by the cataloguer. Emil Orlik is listed as contributor for book design ('Buchausstattung'). The conjunction between 'J. Folnesics' and 'O. Kümmel' in the original entry lacks a comma, which may indicate a transcription issue; both are treated as separate authors of volume 2.

suttinger, johann baptist.original_entry:

Consuetudines Austriacae ad stylus excelsi regiminis infra anasum olim accomodatae par … Nunc vero vero accesserunt additiones praedictarum consuetudinum Austriacarum renovatae: Nec non hujus Juris Austriaci Tractatus, … Authore Der. Pergamentband der Zeit mit einigen Gebrauchsspuren am Rücken. Recht gut erhalten.
suttinger, johann baptist.notes: 1) The entry contains 'Nunc vero vero' — the duplicated 'vero' is an obvious typo; corrected to 'Nunc vero' in the subtitle field (corrected_by_api). 2) The entry uses 'par …' (likely 'par [autorem]') and 'Authore Der. Bernardo Walthero…' with ellipses indicating incomplete names or text; the primary author SUTTINGER is named in the heading, but the contributor Bernardo Walther's full name/title is truncated ('Der.' may be an abbreviation for a title or role). Missing person flag set for the incomplete name following 'par …'. 3) The abbreviation 'Der.' before 'Bernardo Walthero' is ambiguous — it may stand for a religious title (e.g., 'Derselbe', 'Decretorum', or a monastic designation). api_concerned flag set for this ambiguity. 4) Page structure is complex: '1051 pp, 44 nn. Bll, Register und Inhaltsangabe, 104 p, 7 nn. Bll' — these appear to be collation details of a single volume rather than multiple volumes; no single pages value was assigned to avoid misrepresentation. 5) 'anasum' in the title likely refers to 'Anasum' (the Enns river, Latin: Anasus), a known geographic reference in Austrian legal texts — retained as written.

schrötter, franz ferdinand von.original_entry:
TOPOGRAPHIE
Oder kurze Beschreibung desjenigen Distrikts der bayerischen Lande, welchen das durchlauchtigste Erzhaus von Österreich kraft der mit Kuhrpfalz zu Teschen geschlossenen Konvention in Besitz genommen hat. Mit XXI. Kupferstichen und einer Karte versehen. Wien, bey Joseph Edlen von Kurzböck, 1779. Mit mehreren Vignetten, 21 gefalteten Kupfertafeln, 1 gefalteten kolorierten Karte. 8Bll., 64 S, 12 Bll. Originalpappband mit Rückenschild, berieben. Buch und alle Tafeln in sehr gutem Zustand. (Text von Franz Ferdinand von Schrötter. Die Kupfertafeln aus 'Wening: Historico-Topographica Descriptio 1701-1726')
schrötter, franz ferdinand von.notes: The entry notes the text is by Franz Ferdinand von Schrötter, listed here as contributor. The collation '8Bll., 64 S, 12 Bll.' is unusual and not mapped to a simple page count. The copper plates are noted as sourced from 'Wening: Historico-Topographica Descriptio 1701-1726' (Michael Wening's well-known Bavarian topographical work). The authorship attribution to Schrötter is given parenthetically and may warrant verification; no author is formally listed on the title page as described.

weis, johann nepomuk.notes: missing_person: The entry contains 'Herausgegeben von …' with an ellipsis and no following name. WEIS, Johann Nepomuk is listed as the main entry and is likely the editor, but the ellipsis suggests the editor name was intentionally left blank or was unknown to the cataloger. WEIS has been listed as both author and editor as a best-effort interpretation. The note 'Nur Reprints werden angeboten' indicates only reprints are available. The two parts (Theile) are bound in one volume but cataloged with separate years (1856 and 1859); no single publication year is given for the combined volume.

radetzkaja, olga.original_entry:
TOLSTOI, Lew
Kreutzersonate. A.d.Russ. von Olga Radetzkaja. Sofja TOLSTAJA Eine Frage der Schuld. Aus anlass der 'KreutzersonateW von Lew Tolstoi. A.d.Russ von Alfred Frank. Nachworte von Olga Martynova und Oleg Jurjew. Zürich Manesse 2010. 428 S. Oln. m. OU und Lesebändchen.
radetzkaja, olga.notes: This entry appears to be a combined volume containing two works: (1) Lew Tolstoi's 'Kreutzersonate', translated by Olga Radetzkaja; and (2) Sofja Tolstaja's 'Eine Frage der Schuld' (a response to the Kreutzersonate), translated by Alfred Frank. Both works are bound together in one volume (428 S. total). The entry lists two translators for two distinct texts. The main title field captures the first work; Sofja Tolstaja is listed as contributor since her work is the secondary text in the volume. The string 'KreutzersonateW' in the original appears to be a typo for 'Kreutzersonate' (likely OCR or transcription artifact with 'W' appended). Nachworte by Olga Martynova and Oleg Jurjew are not separately captured. Human verification recommended to confirm the combined-volume structure and whether both authors should be listed as co-authors rather than author/contributor.

holub, emil.notes: The entry lists conflicting page counts: '532 S.' appears as a general figure, then 'XVI, 528 S.' for Band 1 and 'IX, 532 S.' for Band 2. It is unclear which page count belongs to which volume definitively. Band 1 page count assigned as 532 (from general figure and Roman numerals XVI + 528 ≈ 544 total), Band 2 as 532 (IX + 532). Additionally, Band 2 is noted as 'doppelt' (two copies present, red edition), and the map in Band 1 is missing. The entry mentions 4 maps in the subtitle but only 3 folded maps are physically present. multiple_editions set true because Band 2 is described as a 'rote Ausgabe' (red edition), suggesting a variant edition is present alongside the main copy.

Band 7 has no page count for the 'Älteste Urkunde' Vierter Theil section. The 'copies' field has been set to 11 reflecting the 11 physical volumes noted, but this may require human verification. problematic_multi_volume: The volume structure is complex — multiple works are bound together within single Bände pairs, page counts are sometimes missing (Band 7–8 for 'Älteste Urkunde' Vierter Theil), and the assignment of content to individual volume numbers within pairs is ambiguous in several cases.

anthing, johann friedrich.original_entry:
ANTHING, (Johann Friedrich)
Collection de cent silhouettes des personnes illustres et celebres dessinees d`apres les originaux. Gotha 1791. Neudruck Weimar Gesellschaft der Bibliophilen 1913. 100 lose Blätter mit ganzseitigen Silhouetten in Lichtdruck. Dazu Broschüre 'Johann Friedrich Anthing. Eine Skizze von Carl Schüddekopf.' In einer bunten Originalkassette. Sehr gut erhalten.
anthing, johann friedrich.notes: multiple_editions: The entry describes the original Gotha 1791 edition and a Neudruck (Weimar, Gesellschaft der Bibliophilen, 1913). Per convention, the first-described edition (Gotha 1791) would normally be the main record, but the physical description (100 loose sheets in Lichtdruck, accompanying Broschüre by Carl Schüddekopf, Originalkassette) clearly belongs to the 1913 Neudruck, which is the copy actually held. The 1913 Neudruck has therefore been used as the main record. The original 1791 Gotha edition is noted here as the source publication. The accompanying Broschüre 'Johann Friedrich Anthing. Eine Skizze von Carl Schüddekopf' is included with the copy.

nabias, s. de.notes: The condition note references Leopold Schönbauer as an NS-Mitglied and directs to Wikipedia for further details. Leopold Schönbauer (1888–1963) was a prominent Austrian neurosurgeon and rector of the University of Vienna; his relationship to National Socialism is historically documented and contested. The cataloger's note is a substantive annotation about the dedicatee's political history that warrants human review before any public use of this record.

ramelli, agostino.original_entry:

ramelli, agostino.notes: The entry mentions 'durch Henning Großen den Jüngern' which refers to the original 1620 German publisher/translator Henning Grosse the Younger. The author Agostino Ramelli (c.1531–c.1600) wrote 'Le diverse et artificiose machine' (1588) in Italian/French; this is a German reprint of the 1620 edition. The author is not explicitly named in the entry — 'Augustini de Ramellis' in the subtitle identifies him. Author has been inferred from world knowledge; flagged for verification. Publisher name 'Vinzentz Verlag' in Hannover is noted but not widely documented for this title.

Sämtliche Werke. Wien Bauer 1816. XXXII, 159 SS. II. Theil 138, 34 gez. S. Mit einem ganzs. Frontispiz vom Dichter und 1 Kpf. Mit Lesebändchen. OLn. Blindprägung m. Rückenvergoldung Hs. Besitzervermerk Wilhelmine Lipschütz. Sehr gut erhalten. (Freund Lessings).
kleist, ewald christian.notes: The entry describes a two-part/two-volume work but the structure is ambiguous: the first part has 'XXXII, 159 SS.' and the second part ('II. Theil') has '138, 34 gez. S.' — it is unclear whether these are two physical volumes or two parts within one volume. Volume 1 page count is given as XXXII + 159 pages but no numeric integer can be cleanly assigned. Parsed as two volumes with page details in notes. Additionally, Ewald Christian von Kleist (1715–1759) was a German poet and friend of Lessing; the parenthetical '(Freund Lessings)' confirms this identification. However, a 'Sämtliche Werke' published in Wien by Bauer in 1816 should be verifiable — the cataloger's note is consistent with known biographical facts. The 'Kpf.' abbreviation (Kupferstich, i.e., copper engraving) is noted in illustrations.

hoffmann, adolf.notes: Die Gesamtausgabe ist auf dem Titelblatt als 'in zwölf Bänden' angekündigt, umfasst aber tatsächlich 15 beschriebene Inhaltsbände plus einen abschließenden Kommentarband (Rommel-Monographie), also 16 Bände insgesamt. Ab Band 6 fehlt der Zusatz 'in zwölf Bänden' auf den Titelblättern. Die Bandnummern sind im Eintrag nicht explizit angegeben; die hier vergebenen Nummern 1–16 folgen der Reihenfolge der Beschreibung. Band 16 ist im Eintrag nicht beschrieben — es ist unklar, ob ein 16. Band existiert oder ob die Zählung bei 15 endet. Der letzte beschriebene Band (Rommel-Monographie, 1930) könnte als Ergänzungsband außerhalb der Hauptreihe stehen. Menschliche Überprüfung der Bandstruktur empfohlen.

neu, till.notes: 1) The subtitle 'Die Salons von 1886-1896' is likely incorrect: Zola's art criticism covered the Salons of the 1860s–1880s, not 1886–1896. radspieler, hans.notes: The volume structure is complex and partially ambiguous. The entry lists 16 line-break-separated content blocks but the mapping of content blocks to volume numbers is not explicitly stated — the assignment here is inferred from the sequence. Band XV is explicitly identified as 'C. M. Wielands Leben von J. G. Gruber', and Band XVI as 'Comische Erzählungen Combabus. Der verklagte Amor.' The phrase 'als Band XVI doppelt' is unclear — it may mean a duplicate copy of Band XVI exists, or that Band XVI is counted twice in the set. The publisher name is not explicitly stated in the entry (only the place 'Hamburg' and year 1984 are given); the publisher is likely 'Hamburger Stiftung zur Förderung von Wissenschaft und Kultur' or a commercial imprint, but cannot be confirmed from the entry alone. The total page count of 'ca. 6400 Seiten' is approximate. The note 'Nr. 5692' suggests a numbered copy of Band XVI specifically.

schaber, professor.original_entry:
BLUMAUER, Alois
Virgils Aeneis travestirt von …Erster Band mit Vignette Frankfurt und Leipzig 1798. 1.-4.Buch 83 S. Zweiter Band mit Vignette Frankfurt und Leipzig 1798. 5.-8.Buch 81 S. Dritter Band mit Vignette Frankfurt und Leipzig 1798. 9. Buch. 84 S.Vierter und letzter Band ...ausgeführt von Professor Schaber. Frankfurt und Leipzig 1794. Mit einer Vignette und einem Prolog an Blumauer und An den Leser vom Verfasser 1793. Zehntes Buch. 10, 116 S. OHLdr. Recht gut erhalten.
schaber, professor.notes: The fourth and final volume (Zehntes Buch) is dated 1794, earlier than the first three volumes (1798), and is stated to have been completed by Professor Schaber — consistent with the known publication history of Blumauer's work, where the final volume was completed posthumously by another hand. However, the year 1794 for the fourth volume predates the 1798 volumes, which is unusual and warrants verification. The entry covers only books 1, 2, 3, 5–10 of the Aeneid (books 4 and 6–9 are grouped into volumes 1–3), but the volume/book mapping is somewhat ambiguous: volume 1 covers books 1–4, volume 2 covers books 5–8, volume 3 covers book 9 only, and volume 4 covers book 10 only — this asymmetry is noted. The '10, 116 S.' for volume 4 likely means 10 preliminary pages plus 116 text pages. Publisher name is not given, only place of publication. Professor Schaber's full name is not provided in the entry.

rölleke, heinz.original_entry:
KINDER- UND HAUSMÄRCHEN
Gesammelt durch die Brüder Grimm. Vollständige Ausgabe auf der Grundlage der dritten Auflage (1837). Herausgegeben von Heinz Rölleke. Frankfurt Deutscher Klassiker Verlag 1999. Sonderausgabe. 1302 S. OPbd. Verlagsfrisch.
DEUTSCHE SAGEN
Herausgegeben von den Brüdern Grimm. Ausgabe auf der Grundlage der ersten Auflage. Ediert und kommentiert von Heinz Rölleke. Sonderausgabe. Frankfurt Deutscher Klassiker Verlag 1999. 1073 S. OPbd. Kassette. Verlagsfrisch. Beide Bände
rölleke, heinz.notes: The entry describes two separate works by the Brüder Grimm, both edited by Heinz Rölleke, both Sonderausgaben published by Deutscher Klassiker Verlag Frankfurt 1999. The first (main record) is 'KINDER- UND HAUSMÄRCHEN' (1302 S., OPbd., verlagsfrisch). The second is 'DEUTSCHE SAGEN' (1073 S., OPbd., Kassette, verlagsfrisch), ediert und kommentiert von Heinz Rölleke. The entry closes with 'Beide Bände', indicating both volumes are owned together. The second work (DEUTSCHE SAGEN) is noted here only; it should ideally receive its own catalogue record. The 'Kassette' notation for the second work likely refers to a slipcase (Kassette = Schuber/Kassette).

löhneisen, wolfgang frhr. von.original_entry:
SCHOPENHAUER, Arthur
Sämtliche Werke. Textkritisch bearbeitet und herausgegeben von Wolfgang Frhr. Von Löhneisen. Frankfurt Cotta – Insel 1976 5. – 6. Tausend Band 1 – 2 von fünf Bänden. Darin Die Welt als Wille und Vorstellung. 731, 853 S. OLn.m.OU. Neuwertig. 1-2
löhneisen, wolfgang frhr. von.notes: The entry states 'Band 1–2 von fünf Bänden' and both volumes contain 'Die Welt als Wille und Vorstellung'. In the standard Cotta/Insel edition of Schopenhauer's Sämtliche Werke edited by Wolfgang Frhr. von Löhneysen, Bands 1 and 2 are indeed both parts of 'Die Welt als Wille und Vorstellung', which is consistent. The editor's name is spelled 'Löhneisen' in the entry but is more commonly rendered 'Löhneysen' — flagged for verification. The trailing '1-2' at the end of the entry appears to be a copy or shelf notation and has been disregarded.

stammler, notker der.notes: missing_person: The entry contains '…Einhard' with an ellipsis before the name, suggesting a role or phrase preceding 'Einhard' is missing or truncated (e.g., 'Zusammen mit' or similar). Einhard has been listed as a contributor, but the exact relationship is unclear. api_concerned: 'Leben und Taten Karls des Grossen' is the standard German title for Einhard's 'Vita Karoli Magni', not a work by Notker der Stammler (whose relevant work is 'Gesta Karoli Magni'). It is possible this volume contains both texts (Notker's and Einhard's accounts), but the authorship attribution to NOTKER DER STAMMLER as primary author is ambiguous and may be incorrect or reflect a combined edition. Human verification recommended.

hölter, achim.notes: The entry is catalogued under PROUST, Marcel, but the title 'Leseerfahrungen deutschsprachiger Schriftsteller von Theodor W. Adorno bis Stefan Zweig' describes a collection of German-language writers' reading experiences — this appears to be an anthology about Proust (or reading in general) edited by Achim Hölter, not a work authored by Proust himself. The listed author may actually be the subject rather than the author of the work. Human verification is needed to determine whether Proust should be listed as author, subject, or not at all.

jennings, h..original_entry:
JENNINGS, H.
Die Rosenkreuzer Ihre Gebräuche und Mysterien. Zwei Bände in einem Band. Wien Amonesta-Verlag. 1920.
1.Band 224 S.
2.Band Mit ausführlichem Namen- und Sachregister. Berlin Barsdorf 1912. IV, 247 S. Beide Bände zusammen mit ca. 300 Illustrationen und 12 Tafeln. Zeitbedingt schlechtes Papier. OLn.
jennings, h..notes: The two volumes are described as bound together ('Zwei Bände in einem Band'), yet they have different publishers and years (Wien, Amonesta-Verlag, 1920 for vol. 1; Berlin, Barsdorf, 1912 for vol. 2). The main record uses the first-described publisher and year. The author 'JENNINGS, H.' likely refers to Hargrave Jennings, the known author of 'The Rosicrucians: Their Rites and Mysteries' — this appears to be a German translation, but no translator is named and is_translation is not explicitly indicated in the entry. api_concerned is set because the entry structure is unusual (two volumes from different publishers/years bound together) and the translation status is ambiguous. problematic_multi_volume is set because the two volumes have different publication details that cannot be cleanly reconciled into a single multi-volume structure.

vancsa, max.notes: corrected_by_api: 'Altertunsvereine' corrected to 'Altertumsvereins' (obvious typo). api_concerned: The entry combines two distinct texts bound together ('2 in einem Band'). The first item is described as a 'Separatabdruck' (offprint) from Band IV of 'Geschichte der Stadt Wien' with no publisher/year given separately; the second item (Wien Holzhausen 1909) appears to be a different work by the same author. It is unclear whether both items share the same publisher and year, or only the second does. The title used as main title is that of the first item, but publisher/year data is only explicitly stated for the second. Human verification recommended. problematic_multi_volume: The two volumes/items are bound together but have different titles and potentially different publication details. Volume 1 lacks explicit publisher and year. The ellipsis ('….') before the second item's description may indicate missing text or a subtitle that was not fully recorded.

ziehlke, friedrich.original_entry:
KAMASUTRAM DES VATSYAYANA
Ungekürzte, vollständige deutsche Ausgabe. Aus dem Sanskrit übertragen von Sir Richard Burton und F.F.Arbuthnot. Deutsch von Eduard Kolb und Julius Weltmann. Anhand des Urtextes rvidiert und mit einem Geleitwort versehen von Friedrich Ziehlke. Verlag Schustek Hanau/Main 1966. Mit zahlr. Abb. 302 S. OLn.m.OU und Schuber. Neuwertig. Mit einer Widmung des Herausgebers an das Ehepaar Ehrenfels.
ziehlke, friedrich.notes: The entry contains the typo 'rvidiert' (missing 'e'), corrected to 'revidiert' in interpretation. The translation chain is complex: Burton and Arbuthnot produced an English translation from Sanskrit; Kolb and Weltmann then translated that into German. Both pairs are listed as contributors accordingly. Friedrich Ziehlke is listed as editor/reviser who compared against the original Sanskrit text and wrote the foreword.

EISLER, Lou
Übersetzerin, Publizistin 1906-1998. Gattin von Hanns Eisler, nach dessen Tod Gattin von Ernst Fischer.
42 zeiliger hs. Brief an Charlotte Eisler vom 5.12.1942 aus Los Angeles in Englisch.
70 zeiliger hs. Brief an Charlotte Eisler vom 1.12.1945. Ein Teil davon an den Maler Georg Eisler, Sohn von Charlotte und Hanns.
3 seitiger hs. Brief an Charlotte und Georg Eisler vom 10. Dezember aus Los Angeles in Englisch
31 zeiliger hs. Brief an Charlotte und Georg Eisler vom 14. Jänner 1946
36 zeiliger hs. Brief an Charlotte Eisler aus NewYork in Englisch von 19.Dez.
2 seitiger ms. Brief an Charlotte Eisler vom 26.3.1939 aus New York mit 2 zeiliger hs. Bemerkung.
41 zeiliger ms. Brief an Charlotte Eisler vom 27. Juni 1939 aus Mexico mit hs. Korrekturen
25 zeiliger ms. Brief an Georg Eisler aus Kalifornien ohne Datum mit hs. Korrekturen.
eisler, lou.notes: This entry describes a collection of 9 autograph letters (handwritten and typewritten) by Lou Eisler, addressed to Charlotte Eisler and/or Georg Eisler, written between 1939 and 1946 from various locations (Los Angeles, New York, Mexico, California). The entry includes biographical context: Lou Eisler (1906–1998), translator and publicist, was the wife of Hanns Eisler and later of Ernst Fischer. The entry is an autograph/manuscript item rather than a conventional bibliographic record; standard bibliographic fields such as publisher, year, and pages are not applicable. api_concerned is set because 'Lou Eisler' as wife of Hanns Eisler is not a widely documented figure under that name — she may be better known as 'Louise Eisler' or under another name variant; human verification of the identity is recommended.

franck, martine.original_entry:
FRANCK, Martine
Fotografin, Mitglied von Magnum 1938-2012. Hs. Brief an Alice Eisler, 8 Zeilen aus Paris 07/02 auf einem Foto der Künstlerin.
Ansichtskarte mit hs. Adresse und 10 zeiligem Text der Fotografin an Alice Eisler vom 6/12/17.
franck, martine.notes: multiple_editions: The entry describes two distinct autograph items — (1) a handwritten letter of 8 lines from Paris, dated 07/02, on a photograph of the artist, addressed to Alice Eisler; and (2) a postcard with handwritten address and 10-line text addressed to Alice Eisler, dated 6/12/17. These are treated as two separate physical objects; the first is used as the main record. api_concerned: Martine Franck (1938–2012) was indeed a Belgian-French photographer and Magnum member, consistent with the entry. However, the date '6/12/17' on the second item postdates her death in 2012, which is contradictory. This may be a cataloging error (possibly the year 2007 or another date) and requires human verification. Additionally, the dates '07/02' and '6/12/17' are ambiguous in format (day/month/year vs. month/year).

Geschichte der Stadt Salzburg. I. Buch Geschichtliche Stadtbeschreibung. Salzburg 1885. II. Buch Zeitgeschichte bis zum Ausgange des 18. Jahrhunderts. Salzburg 1890. II. Buch Zweite Hälfte. zillner, franz valentin.notes: multiple_editions: The entry describes the original 19th-century editions (Salzburg 1885, 1890) alongside a 1985 Neuauflage edited by Heinz Dopsch; the 1985 reprint is treated as the main record per convention. api_concerned: The role of Heinz Dopsch is ambiguous — he is listed as editor of the Neuauflage but could also be the publisher/organizer of the reprint; no publisher name is given for the 1985 edition. problematic_multi_volume: The page counts (VIII, 456 S.; XXV, 357 S.; 362-796 S.) cannot be unambiguously assigned to the three volumes. The third volume is labeled 'II. Buch Zweite Hälfte' and shares the 'II. Buch' designation with volume 2, making the volume numbering structurally unclear.
